summary:
Several household items, including a cane, chair, mirror, and bunk beds, are said to be haunted by malevolent spirits.
These items are believed to be cursed, resulting in the deaths of numerous people who sat or interacted with them.
Examples include the "ghost cane" in Indiana, the "chair of death" in Pennsylvania, and the haunted mirror in Louisiana, which is said to contain the trapped souls of a slave and her family.

A mother and daughter survived a car accident in Melbourne, Australia, that killed three of their children, who were believed to have been eaten by crocodiles by a 5-year-old girl who survived the crash. The children's mother, Akon Guode, was released from police custody after being questioned about the incident, and the family's father, Joseph Manyang, defended her, saying she was innocent and distraught. The investigation is ongoing, with police appealing for information

Cardiothoracic surgeon and TV host Mehmet Oz has successfully defended himself against criticism from 10 doctors who accused him of promoting "quack treatments" for personal gain.
Oz's team responded with an investigative segment, highlighting the critics' own questionable motives and credentials.
The doctors' letter was seen as a failed attempt to discredit Oz, who has a strong media presence and a loyal following.
Columbia University has defended Oz, saying he has the right to express his opinions

Google has updated its Android handsets to include handwriting recognition in 82 languages and 20 scripts.
The feature allows users to draw emojis or write text by hand, and can be used with or without a stylus.
Google has also launched a new tool that allows users to locate a lost phone by searching for "find my phone" on the search engine's homepage.
The tool can pinpoint the phone's location and remotely ring it, but cannot lock or erase its data.

A team of geophysicists has discovered a massive secondary magma chamber beneath Yellowstone National Park, measuring 11,035 cubic miles, which is 11 times larger than the Grand Canyon. The chamber, located 12-28 miles beneath the surface, contains 98% hot rock and 2% molten rock, too deep to cause an eruption. The discovery explains the high levels of carbon dioxide released daily by the supervolcano.

At 80, Jan Bearman got her first tattoo in memory of her daughter Shell, who died of complications from diabetes in 2011.
Jan has since gotten a new tattoo every birthday to commemorate her daughter, with the latest being a coloured shell below her daughter's name.
The tattoos are a memorial for Jan and a way for her to carry her daughter with her, and she says they've made her realize she's not a hypocrite for getting inked herself.
Jan

A 12-year-old King Charles Spaniel named Alfie suffered third-degree burns from a heated pad used during an MRI scan at the Animal Health Trust clinic.
The pad had been warmed in a microwave, but was not checked for temperature before being applied to Alfie's back.
The incident has led to a change in the clinic's practice, with heated pads now being used in an incubator to ensure they are at a safe temperature.
Alfie is receiving treatment for his

Kim Kardashian is saving her luxurious designer wardrobe for her 22-month-old daughter North West, creating an 'archive' of her most iconic outfits. The 34-year-old reality star plans to hand down everything to North, including her Givenchy wedding dress, when she gets older. Kim credits her husband Kanye West for introducing her to the fashion world and wants North to follow in their footsteps, embracing her own unique style.

Bulgaria has been named Europe's cheapest destination for summer holidays, with Black Sea resorts offering the best value in terms of meals and other activities.
The country's strong pound and high inflation have cut the cost of visiting, with a meal for two costing £19.60 and a cup of coffee 78p.
Bulgaria's most popular resort, Sunny Beach, is a carbon copy of those in Spain and Greece, with a long summer season and 300 days of

A 61-year-old US postal worker, Doug Hughes, landed a gyro-copter on the US Capitol lawn to protest against government corruption and campaign finance reform, prompting a lockdown and emergency response.
The pilot, who has been planning the stunt for two years, claimed he informed the Secret Service and Capitol police of his intentions, but authorities were not aware of his flight until it landed.
Hughes faces potential consequences, including losing his job and freedom, but believes his actions will bring

A sheep-herding dog named Stains received a lion-like makeover from its groomers, with half its coat shaved to create a voluminous mane around its head and neck.
The dog's owner was initially surprised but then laughed hysterically at the transformation, capturing the moment on camera.
The lion-like look, complete with an unshaved tail, made Stains look majestic and drew attention from the other dogs in the house.
The video ends with Stains drinking water,

Motherwell will fight the SPFL over free entry to the Premiership play-offs for season-ticket holders if they finish in the play-off place.
The club's general manager, Alan Burrows, says they will not charge season-ticket holders for the play-off final if they make it to the tie.
Rangers are also considering seeking an SPFL exemption to let their 25,000 season ticket holders into play-off games for free.
The SPFL had previously withdrawn a bid to force

Niall Horan will sing on stage with Rory McIlroy at Gillette Stadium in Massachusetts in September.
The One Direction singer will perform with McIlroy after Horan caddied for him at the Masters Par-3 Contest.
Horan revealed the deal was made as part of the Par-3 Contest, where he slipped and fell while caddying for McIlroy.
McIlroy will compete at the Deutsche Bank Championship in Boston around the same time as

A chef visits Grenada, finding inspiration in the island's fresh produce and local recipes.
The island offers a variety of exotic fruits, vegetables, and seafood, making it a culinary paradise.
Grenada's food scene includes traditional dishes like oil down, sushi, and Caribbean specialties like crabback and salt fish fritters.
The island's unique flavors and ingredients make it a must-visit destination for foodies and chefs.

The Eshima Ohashi bridge in Japan is a steep, 1-mile-long bridge with a 6.1% gradient, making it a challenging hill start for drivers.
It's the third-largest rigid frame bridge in Japan and the third-largest in the world, spanning Lake Nakaumi and connecting cities of Matsue and Sakaiminato.
The bridge's steep slope and rollercoaster-like appearance make it a unique and intimidating feature.

A Washington teen, Ibrahim Ahmad, has been suspended from school for five days after wearing a vest filled with paper-tube props to ask his date to prom. Ahmad claims the prank was meant to be a harmless and creative way to ask his date, Rilea Wolfe, but was deemed inexcusable by school officials. He believes the punishment was unfair and felt racist, as he is of Middle Eastern descent. The suspension has ruined Ahmad's prom plans.

A University of South Carolina student has been suspended after a Snapchat photo showed her writing "n*****s" as the reason for the campus's poor Internet connection on a whiteboard.
The student, a former member of the Kappa Delta Chapter sorority, was condemned by fellow students and university officials for using a racist slur.
The incident is the third racially-charged incident on a college campus in the past week, following a noose found at Duke University and racist comments made

Ashley Barnes was sent off for a 'criminal challenge' on Nemanja Matic, sparking a 10-10 match between Everton and Burnley.
Everton won 1-0, with Kevin Mirallas scoring the only goal, and Aaron Lennon was named man of the match.
Burnley's manager Sean Dyche was unhappy with referee Mike Jones' decisions, particularly allowing Mirallas to stay on the pitch for a foul on George Boyd.
The loss leaves Burnley

A 49-year-old grandmother, Patricia Ebel, was arrested after crashing her luxury car into another vehicle in Naples, Florida, while driving under the influence. Ebel failed sobriety tests and refused a breathalyzer test, and is facing DUI charges, including endangering a minor. Neither Ebel nor her 10-year-old grandson was injured in the crash, but her car was totaled. Ebel was taken away by police after her grandson hugged her before being booked.

An 11-year-old boy, Shiva, was beaten to death by his school head teacher for stealing a pencil and eraser from another boy's bag.
The head teacher, Lalit Verma, has been arrested and charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
Shiva's death was reportedly caused by internal bleeding, and his body is undergoing a post-mortem examination.
This incident is not an isolated case, with reports of young Indian boys being beaten to death

Queen Margrethe of Denmark celebrated her 75th birthday with a two-day celebration, including a gala banquet and fireworks display at Fredensborg Castle.
The Danish royal family, including Crown Prince Frederik, Crown Princess Mary, and Prince Joachim, joined European royals from Sweden, Norway, and Belgium for the festivities.
Queen Margrethe was joined on the balcony by her sons and grandchildren, and later appeared at a procession down Copenhagen's main shopping street Strø

Sawyer Sweeten, 19, took his own life while visiting family in Texas, with no apparent warning signs of depression or suicidal thoughts.
His family, including his twin brother Sullivan and sister Madylin, are "devastated" by the news, with his mother Elizabeth sharing a photo of the family on Facebook just days before.
Ray Romano, who worked with Sawyer on Everybody Loves Raymond, called him "a wonderful and sweet kid" and expressed his sadness

The average British worker attends 6,239 meetings in their career, with 60% finding most meetings "pretty pointless". Many workers zone out or fall asleep during meetings, with 70% admitting to constantly zoning out and 1 in 5 confessing to making mistakes due to distraction. The survey also found that 45% of employees feel their company wastes money on travel for meetings, and that conference calls are often just as effective as face-to-face meetings. Workers also

Lewis Hamilton has opened up about the meaning behind his tattoos in an interview with Men's Health magazine.
The Formula One champion has a range of tattoos, including religious images, musical notes, and inspirational quotes, which hold personal significance.
Hamilton emphasizes the physical demands of racing, stating that his heart rate can reach up to 190 beats per minute during qualifying laps.
He also discusses the intense training required to maintain his physical condition, having lost six kilos last year to meet the weight

Amateur astronomer captures image of sun spot resembling a giant yolk.
Sun spot is a common phenomenon caused by magnetic fields and surface temperature reductions.
The sun spot can be hundreds of thousands of miles wide and is visible from Earth without a telescope.
Gordon Ewen, 57, captured the image using his telescope in his garden in Hertfordshire, England.

Photographer James Oatway captured a brutal attack in Johannesburg's Alexandra Township, where a Mozambican man was beaten and stabbed to death in broad daylight.
The attack, which took less than two minutes, was part of a wave of xenophobic violence against foreign immigrants in South Africa.
Oatway's photos of the attack, which were published in the Sunday Times, have sparked outrage and led to the arrest of the four suspects.
The incident is part of a larger problem

Demolition of Archway Sheet Metal Works begins, clearing the way for Tottenham's new stadium development.
The £400 million project aims to create a 56,000-seater stadium with a one-tier stand, set to open in 2018.
Tottenham has overcome a long court battle with the company, which was granted a compulsory purchase order in July 2014.
The new stadium will regenerate the Tottenham area with a central square, shops, housing, and refurbished buildings

Experts provide six signs to spot a potentially toxic partner.
They include lack of self-awareness, emotional unavailability, dishonesty, immaturity, self-loathing, and negativity.
These traits can lead to an unhealthy relationship and emotional exhaustion.
Asking questions can help identify these warning signs in a potential partner.

A bear in Alaska's Katmai National Park was caught on camera letting a salmon slip away after it leaped into the air, allowing the fish to escape.
The salmon, a Sockeye, was migrating upstream to reach its breeding grounds, making it an easy target for the bear.
The bear, however, made a crucial error of judgment, allowing the salmon to continue its journey.
The incident was captured by photographer Juergen Sohns, who said the bear was

ABC is developing a proof of concept for a revival of "The Muppets," with "The Big Bang Theory" co-creator Bill Prady co-writing the script and Bob Kushell serving as showrunner.
The project, if successful, could go straight to series and feature the original Muppet characters, including Kermit the Frog, Fozzie Bear, and Miss Piggy, gathering for a meeting about the new "Muppet Show".
The revival comes as variety

Sinn Fein president Gerry Adams sparked outrage with comments on US TV about the abduction and murder of Jean McConville, describing it as 'what happens in wars'. Relatives of McConville, who were orphaned by her murder, have called Adams a liar and accused him of having 'blood on his hands'. Adams denied any involvement in McConville's death, but admitted to being a senior manager of the IRA. He claimed he reported himself to the police after a

Crystal Palace manager Alan Pardew denies interest in signing Tim Krul from Newcastle.
Pardew says he's happy with current goalkeeper Julian Speroni and has no contact with Newcastle or Krul.
There's been no discussion about a transfer, and Pardew is confident Krul will stay at Newcastle.
Pardew is focused on strengthening the team through other transfers.

The Queen is hiring a sous chef to develop their technical expertise using organic and unique ingredients from her Royal gardens.
The successful applicant will work at Buckingham Palace and other Royal residences, ordering in seasonal ingredients and game from the Queen's private kitchen garden.
The job pays around £28,000-a-year, with accommodation available for a salary adjustment, and includes all meals provided.
The Queen's former personal chef revealed her simple tastes, including Special K, jam sandwiches, and chocolate cake.

The final resting place of Lizzie Borden and her family was vandalized at Oak Grove Cemetery in Fall River, Massachusetts, with black and green paint.
The vandalism occurred after a Lifetime miniseries, The Lizzie Borden Chronicles, premiered on Sunday night, and is believed to be related to the show's debut.
The Borden family burial monument has been defaced before, with previous incidents including graffiti, pennies, flowers, and other items.
The cemetery director is

Young people in Iran are defying the country's hardline Islamic image to create a more Westernized society, with many openly living gay lifestyles, drinking alcohol, and kissing in public.
The Iranian youth, representing over 63% of the population, is among the most politically active groups in the Islamic world and poses a significant threat to the current theocratic rule.
The youth's influence on the Islamic Republic's political agenda since 1997 has been significant, and their demands have

A couple in Oregon has been awarded $240,000 for over a decade of noise complaints from their neighbors' barking Tibetan mastiffs.
The dogs' barking continued until 5am and throughout the day, causing the Kreins significant disquiet.
The jury ruled that the dogs should be debarked within 60 days to reduce the noise.
The defendants' attempt to argue the dogs protected their livestock was rejected by the court.

A driver in Gloucestershire followed his sat nav down a country lane, losing the roof of his lorry suspended in the air between a hedge and a tree. The incident occurred on an unregistered road, and police are considering pursuing the driver for a driving offence. The driver's sat nav led him astray, and he failed to check the height of his vehicle. Police are warning other drivers to be cautious on country roads and to check their vehicle's height.

Rev. Robert H. Schuller, a Southern California televangelist and author, has died at 88 after a long battle with a tumor in his esophagus.
Schuller's ministry, which was once a prominent force in evangelical Protestantism, collapsed in recent years due to a leadership transition and financial struggles.
He was known for his message of "Possibility Thinking" and love of God, which blended elements of Bible and psychology, and had a large following

A light aircraft crashed near Loch Etive in western Scotland, and the bodies of two people were found amid the wreckage this evening.
The plane was reported missing around 30 miles east of Oban, and a rescue operation was launched earlier today.
Helicopters and lifeboats were scrambled to locate the missing plane, which was travelling from central Scotland to Tiree, the most westerly island of the inner Hebrides.
The incident is being investigated, and police are urging

Indiana has seen a significant increase in HIV cases, with 120 confirmed cases and 10 preliminary positive cases in Scott County, following the approval of a short-term needle exchange program by Gov. Mike Pence. The program, which provides clean syringes to intravenous drug users, has been in place for over two weeks and has distributed 5,322 clean syringes to 86 participants. Health officials expect the number of cases to rise as more people are tested, and

Reading goalkeeper Adam Federici's error in the FA Cup semi-final led to his team's defeat and a missed chance to reach the final on May 30, coinciding with his wedding to girlfriend Micaela Gardner in Italy. Federici had already booked his wedding for the weekend of the final, which would have clashed with the event. He apologized to fans for his mistake on Twitter, but is expected to bounce back from the error. Federici's Reading boss, Steve Clarke

The NCAA claims it has no legal responsibility to ensure the academic integrity of courses offered to student-athletes, despite its own rhetoric and established systems of academic eligibility and accountability. The organization argues it has a hands-off approach, likening its role to that of the American Bar Association or American Medical Association. Critics, including former UNC whistleblower Mary Willingham, say this is a contradiction of the NCAA's long-held commitment to the education of student-athletes. The NCAA's stance

A property developer was found guilty of falsely accusing a neighbour of a racist assault and was given a six-month suspended sentence for common assault and perjury. Chaddah had accused Dean Paton of kicking him and using a racist slur, but CCTV footage cleared Paton of any wrongdoing.

The owners of two dogs that mauled a jogger to death in Michigan pleaded no contest to owning a dangerous dog causing death and agreed to 15 years in jail. The dogs, cane corsos, had attacked at least two neighbors before killing 46-year-old Craig Sytsma in July. The owners, Sebastiano Quagliata and Valbona Lucaj, could be deported after serving their sentences. The Sytsma family is satisfied with the plea deal, but

Cesc Fabregas scored a late goal to give Chelsea a 1-0 win over QPR at Loftus Road, moving the team seven points clear at the top of the table.
The win was a vital one for Chelsea, who are playing for the Premier League title.
Fabregas praised the team's mentality and performance, saying they never gave up despite a tough game.
The win marked a significant step towards Chelsea's fifth league title, with Mourinho saying it was

Chelsea is set to face Stoke City at Stamford Bridge on Saturday, with Diego Costa's hamstring injury status uncertain.
Stoke's Jonathan Walters is expected to return from a calf injury, while Victor Moses will not play due to being loaned out to his parent club.
Chelsea has not lost a home match in the league this season, while Stoke has failed to score in six of their last seven Premier League games against Chelsea.

Toby Alderweireld will return to Atletico Madrid after his loan spell at Southampton ends.
The Belgian defender has impressed this season and hinted at a desire to remain in the Premier League.
Atletico Madrid's sporting director Jose Luis Perez Caminero says they are counting on Alderweireld's return to La Liga.

Tottenham will subsidise Emmanuel Adebayor's £100,000-per-week wages to allow him to leave the club this summer.
The striker's wages will be reduced by a percentage next season to facilitate his departure.
Adebayor has made just nine league starts this season and is out of favour under manager Mauricio Pochettino.
The 31-year-old will be made available for loan or a permanent move after entering the final 12 months of his deal

Australian celebrity chef Pete Evans is under attack for promoting a reworked baby milk formula in his cookbook that contains high levels of vitamin A, which experts warn could be dangerous to babies. The formula, called "Happy Tummy Brew," contains almost five times the maximum daily intake of vitamin A for babies. Evans has been promoting the paleo diet, which advocates for a high intake of meat, fish, eggs, and vegetables, and has shared testimonials from people who claim it has helped

A study found that exposure to Fisher-Price magnets in the 1970s and 1980s may have caused a generation to associate certain letters with specific colors.
At least 6% of participants in the study learned their letter-color pairings from the magnets, with some born between 1970 and 1985 having a 15% prevalence of synesthesia.
The study suggests that synesthesia, a neurological condition where two senses are involuntarily joined, can be

A typed surrender order issued by German president Karl Doenitz on May 8, 1945, is being auctioned for £21,000.
The order, which was seized from Luftwaffe chief Robert von Greim when he was arrested, instructed officers to abandon fighting or face the Allies' wrath.
Von Greim, a WWI fighter ace, killed himself rather than be tortured by the Russians after being arrested, and the note is one of the only copies

Juan Mata's season has been revitalized under Louis van Gaal, with the midfielder scoring and inspiring Manchester United to a 4-2 win over Manchester City. Mata had struggled under Jose Mourinho at Chelsea, but has found a new lease on life at Old Trafford. He has formed close bonds with teammates Ander Herrera and David de Gea, and has rediscovered his form after a period of isolation. Mata's return to form has been a welcome boost to United's fortunes

BMW Mini has unveiled concept smart specs, called Augmented Vision goggles, that project virtual displays onto the road for drivers to see directions and other features.
The goggles, designed to give drivers "super-human powers," show navigation information, speed limits, and local landmarks, and can even project an "X-ray" view of external objects concealed by the car.
The smart specs can be worn in and out of the car, and are connected to a smartphone, allowing drivers to receive text

Former paramedic Crsity Collins used ice while driving and treating patients to raise awareness about the dangers of the drug.
Collins, who was addicted to ice and had post-traumatic stress disorder and borderline personality disorder, says she was never offered support despite frequent hospital visits.
She was only offered help after being arrested and directed to the Department of Veterans Affairs, where she received intensive treatment and support.
Collins now advocates for drug testing for emergency responders and others in high-risk professions

Women throughout history have used various methods to beautify themselves, including ancient Egyptian donkey-milk baths, Greek honey and olive oil concoctions, and Indian cow dung and urine treatments.
The use of unusual ingredients, such as nightingale poop, cow dung, and crushed beetles, was common in ancient beauty regimens.
Women in ancient Japan used rice flour to whiten their faces and nightingale poop to cleanse their skin, while Chinese empress Wu Zetian

An Israeli team has developed a 'thermal protection' case called Amited that warns and cools down phones when they overheat.
The case uses micro-fans to blow heat away from the device or heats it using resistance coils if it gets too cold.
Amited syncs with a smartphone over Bluetooth and tracks temperature changes, allowing users to set custom temperatures zones and set alarm thresholds.
The case is set to launch on Indiegogo on April 9 and will be available for

Three men in their 50s from the York area have been arrested on suspicion of murdering missing chef Claudia Lawrence.
The arrests are linked to ongoing investigations into her disappearance, which began in 2013.
Detectives are treating her disappearance as a suspected murder and are seeking to uncover evidence that could lead to her body being found.
The arrests are the latest development in a case that has seen numerous searches, arrests, and releases over the past decade.

Kazakhstan's President Nursultan Nazarbayev has won re-election with 97% of the vote, sparking concerns over the election's legitimacy. Nazarbayev apologized for not allowing a more competitive election, citing the need to avoid appearing undemocratic. The election has been criticized by the West and human rights bodies for Kazakhstan's crackdowns on dissent and lack of a genuine choice of candidates. Nazarbayev's re-election has been welcomed by neighboring Russia and

English model Stephen James, 24, is becoming one of the most in-demand models in the industry with lucrative contracts and runway appearances.
Despite his tough exterior, James is humble and says his tattoos are about expression and staying grounded, not impressing people.
He has recently landed a new campaign with Windsor Smith shoes in Sydney and is excited to take his newfound fame in stride.
James, who is single and recently unattached, is looking forward to his busy schedule and says he plans

Maryland parents Danielle and Alexander Meitiv are suing authorities after their children, Rafi and Dvora, were picked up by police and held for nearly six hours without food.
The Meitivs, who practice "free-range" parenting, claim authorities crossed a line by taking their children without their consent.
The parents were left to panic for hours before being reunited with their children, who were eventually taken to Child Protective Services.
The Meitivs vow to pursue

Aritz Aduriz scored a 90th-minute equaliser for Athletic Bilbao to deny Valencia victory. The draw took Valencia above Sevilla into fourth place in the pursuit of Champions League qualification. Getafe moved 10 points clear of the bottom three with a 1-0 win over Elche. Espanyol beat Villarreal 3-0 with two goals from Felipe Caicedo, extending Villarreal's winless streak to six games.

Italian navy has retaken control of a fishing boat seized by gunmen off Sicily's coast.
The Airone, carrying 7 fishermen, was intercepted 90km from Libya's Misrata port.
Rifle shots were accidentally fired, injuring one fisherman, and a Libyan soldier was taken into custody.
The incident highlights the ongoing threat of migrant smuggling and violence in the Mediterranean.

Jason McDonald, 34, from Colorado, walked a slackline over a pool containing over 50 hungry alligators at the Colorado Gator Farm.
McDonald, a volunteer and handler of alligators for over 10 years, thought this would be an interesting new way to interact with the creatures.
He said the alligators were surprisingly calm and didn't seem to bother him as he navigated the thin rope line.
McDonald plans to continue his daredevil stunts, including

Twitter suspended over 10,000 accounts linked to Islamic State militants and their supporters in a 24-hour period.
The accounts were removed due to tweeting violent threats and promoting ISIS propaganda.
The move is part of Twitter's efforts to combat the spread of hate speech and violent rhetoric online.
However, experts say the issue is a "whack-a-mole" problem, as banned users quickly set up new accounts.

Jade Wimsey, 24, from Yorkshire, ballooned to a size 20 after consuming up to 7 energy drinks a day, costing £11,000 in 4 years.
She shed 6 stone in 18 months by quitting energy drinks and adopting a healthy diet, reaching a slim size 12.
Her addiction led to cruel comments and low self-esteem, but quitting energy drinks gave her newfound confidence and energy.
Jade now runs a blog to help

Graeme Finlay, 53, is accused of attacking an elderly couple on a luxury cruise ship, claiming he acted in self-defense after being attacked by the husband, Ron Phillips, wielding his crutch.
Finlay said he was ignored by the couple when he joined their table for dinner and became aggressive after Phillips reacted to his comment about the awkward silence.
Finlay denies charges of unlawful wounding and assault causing grievous bodily harm, claiming he only swung his arms to

Two brothers, Jeff and Joey Stallings, were hospitalized after taking a 'bad batch' of synthetic marijuana in Mississippi.
The brothers, in their twenties, fell seriously ill with hallucinations, vomiting, and violent shaking, and were placed in medically-induced comas.
They have since recovered, but their mother, Karen, is warning others about the dangers of synthetic marijuana, which can cause severe symptoms and permanent damage.
The brothers are now facing serious medical issues, including addiction and permanent

Liverpool winger Jordan Ibe is set to return to action after a six-week absence due to a knee injury.
Ibe has been training at a high level and will be included in the squad to face Newcastle on Monday.
Manager Brendan Rodgers is optimistic about Ibe's return, describing him as "absolutely outstanding" in training.
The team will be without Mario Balotelli, who is still recovering from illness, and Steven Gerrard, who remains suspended.

Colin Heath, 43, was jailed for three years and two months for manslaughter after admitting to violently shaking a baby girl, Kealeigh-Anne Woolley, who was left permanently disabled and died 11 years later. The 7-month-old girl suffered severe brain injuries and was left blind, unable to talk, and severely brain damaged. Her mother, Amanda Woolley, described a lifetime of pain and anguish in her victim impact statement.

Robert Dellinger, a former Fortune 500 executive, was sentenced to 20 years in prison for causing a fatal car crash that killed a Vermont couple and their unborn baby in 2013. He pleaded guilty to negligent homicide and assault, and his lawyer said he may petition to have one of the consecutive sentences suspended. Dellinger's remorse was evident during the sentencing hearing, where he apologized for his actions and expressed deep regret for the loss of life. The family of the victims

The bilby, a native Australian marsupial, is under threat due to low numbers, with only small populations remaining in Queensland, the Northern Territory, and Western Australia.
The Greater Bilby, once occupying 70% of the mainland, is now extinct in New South Wales, Victoria, and South Australia, with the Lesser Bilby believed to be extinct.
Predators like feral cats and foxes, as well as habitat destruction and competition with farm animals, are major

A massive underground city has been discovered in Turkey's Cappadocia region, consisting of 3.5 miles of tunnels, secret churches, tombs, and safe havens. 
The subterranean city, dating back around 5,000 years, was built from volcanic ash rock and is believed to have been used as a hideout during the Byzantine era.

Photographer Anthony Barbour, 33, creates mind-bending 360-degree images of British locations by taking 50 pictures and stitching them together.
He uses a Canon 1100d DSLR and PhotoShop to create surreal images of parks, villages, beaches, and other locations.
Barbour finds inspiration in open spaces with interesting landscapes and spends hours rotating in one spot to capture the perfect shot.
The images showcase his creativity and artistic skills, serving as an escape from his

A fireball that exploded over Russia's Kola Peninsula last year shares an orbit with a large asteroid, 2014 UR116, which is due to pass by the moon in 2017.
Researchers used computer models to recreate the fireball's trajectory and found a connection to the Annama meteorite, a 1,100lb rock.
The asteroid, 2014 UR116, poses no immediate threat but could theoretically hit Earth, and its size and orbit make it

A bomb technician created beeping Easter eggs for blind children to hunt, allowing them to participate in the traditional activity.
The device, developed by David Hyche, uses a transmitter to produce a high-pitched beeping sound.
Blind children in Birmingham, Alabama, celebrated Easter by filling baskets with the beeping eggs and trading them in for candy.
The Alabama Institute for Deaf and Blind partnered with Hyche to share the idea with children with disabilities.

An 8-year-old abuse survivor, Marie Surprenant, wrote a heartfelt letter to her social workers and detectives thanking them for helping her find a happy home after being severely beaten as an infant.
Marie, who is now a straight A student, credits her social workers for giving her a safe and loving environment.
Her adoptive mother Michele, also a social worker, said Marie's letter is "living proof that'social work works'" and that her daughter's future

Freddie Gray, 25, died a week after being taken into police custody in Baltimore, with unclear circumstances surrounding his death.
Attorney William Murphy Jr. alleges police are involved in a cover-up, claiming Gray was chased and arrested without probable cause.
Gray's family and supporters are demanding answers, with protests held in Baltimore over the weekend.
Police are declining to release details, citing ongoing investigations, but have placed the officers involved on administrative duties.

Jameela Jamil had 10 amalgam fillings removed and replaced with white fillings after experiencing health problems for nearly seven years.
She believed the fillings were causing her symptoms, including fatigue, headaches, and feeling 'rotten'.
However, experts say the amounts of mercury from fillings are'minuscule' and there is no evidence to suggest they are a health risk.
Jamil remains convinced that the mercury was the cause of her problems, despite medical

Cities around the world have unique nicknames that reflect their history, industry, and environment. Research by Just The Flight found that nicknames like "The Big Apple" for New York and "The City of Love" for Paris have origins that date back to the cities' founding and early development. Some nicknames, like "Sin City" for Las Vegas, have origins that pre-date the city's modern form. The nicknames often reveal interesting and surprising facts about the cities'

Richie Benaud, a legendary Australian cricketer and television commentator, has died at the age of 84 after a battle with skin cancer.
Benaud was a skilled player who won 63 Test caps and took 248 wickets, but he is best known for his dry wit and style as a commentator.
He was a pioneer in changing the way cricket was presented on television, and his influence extended beyond the sport to improve the lives of cricketers through

Michelle Knight, one of the three women held captive by Ariel Castro for almost 10 years, has spoken out about her healing process and refuses to be considered a victim, saying she wants to "overcome and flourish" from her experiences.

A Canadian nurse, Joanna Flynn, has been charged with manslaughter and criminal negligence after allegedly turning off a patient's life support without permission. A man fell asleep on the baggage conveyor at Domodedovo Airport in Russia.

A banyan tree in southern China has a unique, web-like structure of roots that has captured the attention of locals.
